queries: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عَامًا فَأَخَذَهُمُ الطُّوفَانُ وَهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ
وَنُوحًا إِذْ نَادَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ فَاسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُ فَنَجَّيْنَاهُ وَأَهْلَهُ مِنَ الْكَرْبِ الْعَظِيمِ
فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ وَأَصْحَابَ السَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْنَاهَا آيَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ
ونوحا إذ نادى من قبل فاستجبنا له
الأنبياء 76 ونوحا إذ نادى من قبل
هود 40 وما آمن معه إلا قليل
نوح 5 قال رب إني دعوت قومي ليلا ونهارا

import re

text_path = '/mnt/data/file-DQu4hxdRz8yHfKFzBERbXW-text_to_analyse.txt'
text = open(text_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()

# Find spans inside standard double quotes "
double_quote_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'\"(.*?)\"', text, flags=re.DOTALL):
    inner = m.group(1)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    double_quote_spans.append((start, end, inner))
len(double_quote_spans), double_quote_spans[:10]

# Find spans inside Arabic ornate parentheses ﴿...﴾
bracket_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'﴿(.*?)﴾', text, flags=re.DOTALL):
    inner = m.group(1)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    bracket_spans.append((start, end, inner))
len(bracket_spans), bracket_spans[:10]

candidates = []

# From double quotes: pick only the Quran ones (we'll filter manually by inspecting content)
for start, end, inner in double_quote_spans:
    candidates.append((start, end, inner, 'double'))

# From brackets: further split where necessary
for start, end, inner in bracket_spans:
    candidates.append((start, end, inner, 'bracket'))

candidates

import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-DQu4hxdRz8yHfKFzBERbXW-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

# Helper to create span dict
def make_span(start, end, span_text):
    assert text[start:end] == span_text, "Span text mismatch at indices"
    return {"span_start": start, "span_end": end, "span_text": span_text}

spans = []

# 1) Main quote of 29:14
start, end, inner = double_quote_spans[0][0], double_quote_spans[0][1], double_quote_spans[0][2]
spans.append(make_span(start, end, inner))

# 2) Main quote of 21:76
start, end, inner = double_quote_spans[2][0], double_quote_spans[2][1], double_quote_spans[2][2]
spans.append(make_span(start, end, inner))

# 3) Main quote fragment of 54:10
start, end, inner = double_quote_spans[3][0], double_quote_spans[3][1], double_quote_spans[3][2]
spans.append(make_span(start, end, inner))

# 4) Bracket 1 split into two spans around "(٥)"
b1_start, b1_end, b1_inner = bracket_spans[0]
# Find the position of (٥)
idx = b1_inner.find('(٥)')
before = b1_inner[:idx].rstrip()
after = b1_inner[idx+len('(٥)'):].strip()
# Compute absolute indices
before_start = b1_start + b1_inner.find(before)
before_end = before_start + len(before)
after_start = b1_start + b1_inner.find(after, idx+len('(٥)'))
after_end = after_start + len(after)
spans.append(make_span(before_start, before_end, before))
spans.append(make_span(after_start, after_end, after))

# 5) Bracket 2 split into two: find 'فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ'
b2_start, b2_end, b2_inner = bracket_spans[1]
split_key = 'فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ'
idx2 = b2_inner.find(split_key)
part1 = b2_inner[:idx2].rstrip()
part2 = b2_inner[idx2:].strip()
p1_start = b2_start + b2_inner.find(part1)
p1_end = p1_start + len(part1)
p2_start = b2_start + b2_inner.find(part2, idx2)
p2_end = p2_start + len(part2)
spans.append(make_span(p1_start, p1_end, part1))
spans.append(make_span(p2_start, p2_end, part2))

# 6) Bracket 3: omit ellipsis ' ... '
b3_start, b3_end, b3_inner = bracket_spans[2]
# Find the last occurrence of the actual text after ellipsis
ellipsis = ' ... '
pos = b3_inner.find(ellipsis)
if pos != -1:
    part = b3_inner[pos+len(ellipsis):].strip()
    p_start = b3_start + b3_inner.find(part, pos+len(ellipsis))
    p_end = p_start + len(part)
else:
    part = b3_inner.strip()
    p_start = b3_start + b3_inner.find(part)
    p_end = p_start + len(part)
spans.append(make_span(p_start, p_end, part))

len(spans), spans

import json

# Build the citations list
citations = []

def add_aya(span, sura_name, sura_number, aya_number, corrected_text):
    entry = {
        "span_start": span["span_start"],
        "span_end": span["span_end"],
        "span_text": span["span_text"],
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,  # conservative
        "aya_reference": {
            "sura_name": sura_name,
            "sura_number": sura_number,
            "aya_number": aya_number
        },
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": corrected_text,
        "completely_invalid": False
    }
    citations.append(entry)

# Map spans indexes to references
# spans order:
# 0: 29:14
add_aya(spans[0], "العنكبوت", 29, 14, "وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عَامًا فَأَخَذَهُمُ الطُّوفَانُ وَهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ")

# 1: 21:76
add_aya(spans[1], "الأنبياء", 21, 76, "وَنُوحًا إِذْ نَادَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ فَاسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُ فَنَجَّيْنَاهُ وَأَهْلَهُ مِنَ الْكَرْبِ الْعَظِيمِ")

# 2: 54:10 partial
add_aya(spans[2], "القمر", 54, 10, "فَدَعَا رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانتَصِرْ")

# 3: 71:5
add_aya(spans[3], "نوح", 71, 5, "قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي دَعَوْتُ قَوْمِي لَيْلًا وَنَهَارًا")

# 4: 71:6
add_aya(spans[4], "نوح", 71, 6, "فَلَمْ يَزِدْهُمْ دُعَائِي إِلَّا فِرَارًا")

# 5: 29:14 again
add_aya(spans[5], "العنكبوت", 29, 14, "وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عَامًا فَأَخَذَهُمُ الطُّوفَانُ وَهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ")

# 6: 29:15
add_aya(spans[6], "العنكبوت", 29, 15, "فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَأَصْحَابَ السَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْنَاهَا آيَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ")

# 7: 11:40
add_aya(spans[7], "هود", 11, 40, "حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَ أَمْرُنَا وَفَارَ التَّنُّورُ قُلْنَا احْمِلْ فِيهَا مِنْ كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَنْ سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ الْقَوْلُ وَمَنْ آمَنَ ۚ وَمَا آمَنَ مَعَهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ")

# Package top-level JSON
result = {"citations": citations}
print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

آمَنَ ۚ وَمَا آمَنَ مَعَهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ","completely_invalid":false}]}

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
